channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rappler invites women to share experiences of everyday misogyny in upcoming community chat amid recent congressional sexism controversy
item.description: The article announces a community discussion event amid ongoing sexism in the Philippines. It highlights irony that misogyny persists as Women's Month begins, spotlighting recent controversy where Que...

item.title: Southern Ontario braces for heavy rain and potential flooding
item.description: Southern Ontario is facing a period of heavy rainfall that has prompted authorities to raise concerns about possible flooding in several communities, particularly within the Grand River watershed. Res...

item.title: Australia’s fuel security faces pressure amid global oil supply risks
item.description: Australia relies heavily on imported oil products, with over 90 per cent of petrol and diesel refined overseas. Recent geopolitical tensions, including Iran’s commitment to maintain a blockade of the ...

item.title: Bengaluru and Shivamogga Hotels Face Shortage of Commercial LPG Cylinders
item.description: Hotels and restaurants in Bengaluru and Shivamogga are currently facing a shortage of commercial LPG cylinders, largely attributed to the ongoing conflict in West Asia involving Israel, the US, and Ir...

item.title: UN Commends Tanzania for Efforts to Restore Peace After 2025 Elections
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Tanzania has been praised internationally for the serious steps it took to restore peace and stability after the disturbances around the October 29, 2025 General Election. This recogni...

item.title: TIEZA Requests Travel Tax Abolition to Begin in January 2027 Due to Budget Concerns
item.description: The Tourism Infrastructure and Enterprise Zone Authority (TIEZA) has requested the Philippine Senate to postpone the implementation of the proposed travel tax abolition until January 2027. During the ...

item.title: Supreme Court Upholds Integrity of Judicial Officers in West Bengal Voter Roll Revision
item.description: The Supreme Court of India, in a hearing on 10 March 2026, strongly warned petitioners against questioning the integrity of judicial officers overseeing the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of elector...

item.title: Brisbane introduces PayStay app alongside existing CellOPark for parking payments
item.description: Brisbane City Council will launch a new parking app, PayStay, from March 17, offering motorists an additional digital payment option alongside the existing CellOPark system. The free app requires user...

item.title: Study Finds Significant Acceleration in Global Warming Since 2015
item.description: A recent study from the Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research (PIK) has identified a notable increase in the rate of global warming over the past decade. By adjusting temperature records to ac...

item.title: Kenya Convicts 49 Police Officers for Violence Against Protesters, Compensation Panel to Proceed
item.description: President William Ruto has confirmed that 49 police officers have been convicted for using excessive force against civilians during peaceful protests held in 2024 and 2025. He disclosed this during a ...

item.title: POWA Gives Support to Widows, Girls and Police Families in Abuja and Lagos
item.description: For International Women’s Day 2026, the Police Officers Wives Association (POWA) show plenty love to over 400 families of junior police officers plus other community members for Abuja. The programme w...

item.title: Limpopo police investigate multiple murders linked to insurance fraud
item.description: Police in Limpopo have broadened their investigation into a serious insurance fraud scheme allegedly led by former police officer Rachel Shokane-Kutumela. The scheme is reported to have involved the m...

item.title: Guide to the Best Mattress and Bedding Discounts Across Canada
item.description: As Canadians continue to rank among the most sleep-deprived populations globally, upgrading your mattress and bedding can have a major impact on improving sleep quality. With end-of-season winter sale...

item.title: Sindh Government Announces 15-Day Spring Break for Schools and Colleges Amid Fuel Crisis
item.description: KARACHI: The Sindh government has decided that all schools and colleges across the province will remain closed for fifteen days from 16th to 31st March. This step was confirmed by Provincial Minister ...

item.title: Tips for Choosing Comfortable and Traditional Eid Outfits for Children
item.description: Eid is a joyful time, especially when shopping for children's festive clothes like small kurtas, kameezes, and twirly dresses that make them look adorable. However, the most important thing is to prio...

item.title: Factors Influencing Potential Early Spanish Elections in 2026
item.description: The Spanish government, led by Pedro Sánchez, is facing mounting political and geopolitical pressures that could trigger early general elections before June 30, 2026. In Catalonia, Salvador Illa's PSO...

item.title: MPs vote against an outright social media ban for under-16s, favouring flexible regulation
item.description: MPs in the UK Parliament have voted against implementing an Australia-style blanket ban on social media use for under-16s, instead favouring flexible regulatory powers for ministers. The proposal had ...

item.title: Rebuilding Australia’s Public Service Requires More Than Cutting Outsourced Roles
item.description: The Albanese government’s move to reduce reliance on external contractors and consultants is a positive step for the Australian Public Service (APS), reversing a trend that had cost the Commonwealth $...

item.title: Public scepticism and challenges of decentralising police in Nigeria
item.description: For years, Nigerians don dey use the centralised police system as if na necessary evil, especially after the civil war, but e don clear say the system no dey work well for di people. The 2023 World In...

item.title: Vamsadhara Lift Irrigation Project Expected to Benefit Over One Lakh Acres by April
item.description: The Srikakulam district administration is making concerted efforts to complete the Vamsadhara Lift Irrigation Scheme (LIS) by April 2026. This project, located in Hiramandalam, aims to provide water t...
